mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the number of housing starts signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is a leading indicator of economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market, which is a leading indicator of wage growth and inflation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the number of housing starts and a 2-month increase in the number of payroll employees signals a strengthening demand for housing and labor, which are leading indicators of economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 10% in 3 months and PAYEMS rises > 1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the industrial production index signals a strengthening supply chain, which is a leading indicator of economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the consumer price index signals an easing monetary cycle and increasing inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls < 2% in 3 months and CPIAUCSL rises > 2% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the number of housing starts and a 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening demand for housing and labor, which are leading indicators of economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 10% in 2 months and UNRATE falls < 3.5% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
